title: Neptune Fountain - FoUG - Friends of the Uffizi Gallery
highlights: Gardens! The Neptune Fountain, Boboli Gardens is our latest community restoration project. Each year over a million visitors admire this sculpture by Stoldo Lorenzi of Neptune stopped in action of thrusting his trident, created in 1565 for the Cosimo I De Medici. The fountain is in the basin of Neptune. As you can see the fountain is in desperate need of restoration, and we are in the midst of an active drive to raise funds to have the sculpture brought back to a stable and aesthetically pleasing  ...    4 Leonardo donors are needed along with our Friends of the Uffizi Community to restore the fountain. Special thanks to Joseph C. Raskaus

title: The Fountain of the Fork (Boboli Gardens, Florence)
highlights: this article we analyze Boboli’s most famous spouting fountain—Stoldo Lorenzi’s bronze of Neptune clutching his three-pronged trident. Irreverently known by locals as “The Fountain of the Fork,” this water feature was considered the central point of the  ...    The bronze Mannerist statue in the center of the basin represents Neptune wielding his trident, the “fork” for Florentines. The fountain was created in 1565 by sculptor Stoldo Lorenzi for Duke Cosimo I de’ Medici, and a nursery once surrounded it. In the eighteenth century, the nearby area was renovated with the creation of the Basin of Neptune, which had to  ...    Perhaps the nickname chosen by the Florentines for the famous fountain is also a sign of pride: according to tradition,  ...    The location of the Basin of Neptune and the Fountain of the Fork is not the original one that Niccolò Tribolo had in  ...
